title: asplenium cuneifolium subsp. woronowii
intro: Asplenium cuneifolium subsp. woronowii is a subspecies of the Asplenium cuneifolium plant, commonly known as the Wedge-leaved spleenwort, a type of fern native to Eastern Asia. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Asplenium cuneifolium subsp. woronowii include the potential treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and reduce oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves are typically dried and then used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
